name: Al Lucas
abstract: Albert Bennington Lucas (November 16, 1912 – June 19, 1983) was a Canadian jazz double-bassist. Lucas took piano lessons as a child from his mother, Francis Bradley Lucas, a concert pianist, eventually switching to bass and tuba at age 12.

concerts.label: Fred Robbins One-Nite Stand: Illinois Jacquet and His Orchestra with Ella Fitzgerald

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: Then there was another session with Jack (the Bear) Parker replacing Best and Al Lucas in place of Robinson on bass.

about_quotes.speaker: JJ Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: the bass player – Luke, something Lucas [Al Lucas] – a good bass player. We played a lot of theaters.
about_quotes.doc_title: JJ Johnson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: It was J. J., Joe Newman, Sir Charles, Al Lucas, Shadow Wilson... I had done quite a bit of research... I had done so many recordings with Jacquet Jacquet and Dizzy also... in that band was Leo Parker and Sir Charles Thompson, Joe Newman and Russell Jacquet, and J. J. Johnson, Al Lucas on bass

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: He had Junior Ragland and Al Lucas, but Junior Ragland would get drunk, so Duke said, 'I'd better hire another bass player so I can be sure I have one.'

about_quotes.speaker: Percy Heath
about_quotes.evidence: Illinois Jacquet, he had Shadow Wilson, Al Lucas, John Lewis on piano.
about_quotes.doc_title: Percy Heath — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
